title: Financial Industry / Wall Street Political Spending

fire_sector_lobbying.nar_2024: $86.3M (most of any single organization in America)

dodd_frank_deregulation.during_passage: 3,000 lobbyists (6 per member); banks spent $27.3M in 3 months (summer 2010); 1,172 clients listed Dodd-Frank on disclosures
dodd_frank_deregulation.post_passage: Commercial banks spent $55M (2010), $61M (2011), $61M (2012) — spending AFTER passage exceeded DURING
dodd_frank_deregulation.volcker_rule: Goldman estimated $3.7B annual revenue loss; lobbied for exemptions
dodd_frank_deregulation.swaps_pushout_repeal_2014: Repealed via spending bill; Warren: 'written by lobbyists for Citigroup'; Dimon personally called legislators
dodd_frank_deregulation.2018_rollback: $400M+ lobbying (2017-2018); raised too-big-to-fail threshold from $50B to $250B; SVB CEO Becker lobbied for it — SVB collapsed 2023

fairshake_pac.share_of_corporate_dollars: Nearly half of all corporate dollars in 2024 election

carried_interest_loophole.sinema_case: $500K+ from PE executives; demanded $14B closure be removed from IRA as price of vote
carried_interest_loophole.2017_tax_bill: Blackstone, Carlyle, KKR combined $1.31M to GOP; loophole preserved

private_equity_hedge_funds.pe_housing: Blackstone pioneered buy-to-rent (Invitation Homes $18B+); Sun Belt: 1/3 of homes bought by institutional investors
private_equity_hedge_funds.pe_healthcare: $1T in healthcare acquisitions past decade; FTC/DOJ/HHS joint inquiry 2024; 10+ states considering PE regulation

sec_revolving_door.2001_2010: 419 former SEC employees filed 1,949 disclosure statements; 21 filed within 1 week; 2 after just 2 days
sec_revolving_door.enforcement_impact: Regulators 44.7% less likely to initiate enforcement against lobbying banks (IMF 2019)
sec_revolving_door.crypto_revolving_door: SEC crypto enforcement staff switching to companies they regulated
notable: Mary Jo White: Debevoise → SEC Chair → Debevoise
Jay Clayton: Sullivan & Cromwell → SEC Chair

2008_crisis.predatory_lending: Countrywide $20.5M + Ameriquest $8.7M (2002-2006) to defeat anti-predatory lending laws
2008_crisis.bailout_lobbying: Companies receiving $295B in TARP had spent $114M on lobbying + contributions
2008_crisis.academic_finding: Lobbying lenders originated riskier loans, securitized more, had higher delinquencies; politically connected got better bailout deals

party_split.2024_pacs: $78.2M to Dem committees vs $89.6M to GOP committees

subsectors.crypto: Bipartisan by design, but net effect punished crypto-skeptical progressives

cfpb_assault.chamber_2011: ~$30M lobbying with 21 lobbyists to restructure CFPB
cfpb_assault.mulvaney_2017: Requested $0 budget; dropped payday lender lawsuits; received $55K from payday industry
cfpb_assault.vought_2025: Ordered all CFPB activities to cease Feb 14, 2025; federal judges blocked shutdown
cfpb_assault.consumer_cost: $15B+ from weakened oversight ($5B overdraft + $10B credit card late fees)

academic_research.imf_2009_2011: Lobbying lenders took more risk, had higher delinquency, got better bailouts — 'political influence contributed to financial crisis'
academic_research.imf_2019: Regulators 44.7% less likely to enforce against lobbying banks; lobbying banks underperform peers
academic_research.uchicago_2014: Lobbying + network connections positively associated with legislators changing positions toward deregulation
academic_research.georgetown_2019: 'Unexplored risk of capturing the SEC' through revolving door
